walkthroughs.title: The Microsoft Quantum Development Kit
walkthroughs.description: Getting started with the Microsoft Quantum Development Kit in VS Code

steps.title: Welcome to the Microsoft Quantum Development Kit
steps.description:
The Microsoft Quantum Development Kit (QDK) is an open-source SDK that you can use to write quantum programs and execute them on quantum hardware. This walkthrough will show you how to get started with the Microsoft Quantum Development Kit in VS Code.

The QDK gives you rich editor support for writing quantum programs, such as error checking, signature help, completion lists, safely renaming identifiers, and much more.

steps.title: Debug Q# code
steps.description: With your Q# code open in the editor, use the F5 shortcut or the top right icons in the code editor to run or debug the code.

steps.title: Run quantum simulations
steps.description: You can run quantum simulations directly in VS Code and see the program output in the integrated terminal.

steps.title: Run on Azure Quantum
steps.description: If you have an Azure subscription, you can connect to your Azure Quantum workspace and submit your Q# program directly to quantum hardware

steps.title: Starting points
steps.description: Explore Q# safely by opening files in the [Q# playground](command:qsharp-vscode.openPlayground), or work in Python by [creating a Jupyter Notebook](command:qsharp-vscode.createNotebook) from a template

maxOperations.description: The maximum number of operations to include in the circuit diagram.

groupByScope.description: Group operations based on the structure of the original source code.

generationMethod.description: The method to use for generating the circuit diagram. 'static' uses static analysis and partial evaluation, 'classicalEval' uses only classical evaluation, and 'simulate' uses quantum simulation.

sourceLocations.description: Show the source code locations of operations and qubit declarations in the circuit diagram.

Q#.simulation.pauliNoise.markdownDescription:
The Pauli noise to apply when running multiple shots via the Histogram command. This is applied for every gate or measurement on all qubits referenced.

Probability values are in the range [0, 1].

X.description: The probability of a bit flip error occurring

Y.description: The probability of a bit-and-phase flip error occurring

Z.description: The probability of a phase flip error occurring

Q#.simulation.qubitLoss.markdownDescription:
The probability of a qubit loss occurring when running multiple shots via the Histogram command. This is applied on every gate or measurement on all qubits referenced.

Probability values are in the range [0, 1].
Q#.simulation.qubitLoss.description: The probability of a qubit loss occurring

Q#.dev.showDevDiagnostics.description: Show dev diagnostics in Problems view for each open document. This is for internal development and testing purposes.

Q#.notifications.suppressUpdateNotificat.description: Suppress notifications about new QDK updates. If true, you will not be prompted about new features after updates.

Q#.azure.uploadSupplementalData.description: Upload supplemental input data (circuit diagram) to the storage container when submitting jobs to Azure Quantum.

Q#.azure.quantumOsRoot.description: The root URL of the Quantum OS web portal, used when generating deep links to V2 workspaces and jobs.

resourceLangId == qsharp || resourceLangId == openqasm || resourceLangId == qsharpcircuit

viewsWelcome.contents:
Connect to Azure Quantum
[Connect to an existing workspace](command:qsharp-vscode.workspacesAdd)
Note: For the first workspace added there may be several consent prompts to grant VS Code access.
For more information about the QDK and Azure Quantum, visit [https://aka.ms/AQ/Documentation](https://aka.ms/AQ/Documentation).

Welcome to the Quantum Katas — a free, hands-on introduction to quantum computing with Q#.

Each kata is a self-contained tutorial that teaches a concept through short readings followed by exercises you complete in your editor. Topics include qubits, gates, measurement, entanglement, and famous algorithms like Grover's and Deutsch–Jozsa.

No prior quantum experience required.
[$(sparkle) Get started](command:qsharp-vscode.katasContinue)

program.description: Absolute path to a source file.

trace.description: Enable logging of the Debug Adapter Protocol.

showCircuit.description: Display the quantum circuit diagram while running.

languageModelTools.name: azure-quantum-get-jobs
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get a list of recent jobs that have been run by the customer, along with their statuses, in the currently active workspace. Call this when you need to know what jobs have been run recently or need a history of jobs run, for example when a customer asks 'What are my recent jobs?'

languageModelTools.name: azure-quantum-get-job
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the job information for a customer's job given its id. Call this whenever you need to know information about a specific job, for example when a customer asks 'What is the status of my job?'

languageModelTools.name: azure-quantum-connect-to-workspace
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Starts the UI flow to connect to an existing Azure Quantum Workspace. Call this when the customer does not have an active workspace, and agrees to connect to a workspace.

languageModelTools.name: azure-quantum-download-job-results
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Download and display the results from a customer's job given its id. Call this when you need to download or display as a histogram the results for a job, for example when a customer asks 'What are the results of my last job?' or 'Can you show me the histogram for this job?'

languageModelTools.name: azure-quantum-get-workspaces
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get a list of workspaces the customer has access to, in the form of workspace ids. Call this when you need to know what workspaces the customer has access to, for example when a customer asks 'What are my workspaces?'

languageModelTools.name: azure-quantum-submit-to-target
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Submits a Q# or OpenQASM program to Azure Quantum. The path to a .qs or .qasm file must be specified in the `filePath` parameter. The program will first be compiled to Quantum Intermediate Representation (QIR), and then submitted to run on the specified target.

filePath.description: The absolute path to the .qs or .qasm file. If this file is part of a project, the whole project will be compiled as the program. A .qs file belongs to a project if it resides anywhere under a src/ directory whose parent directory also contains a qsharp.json manifest.

targetId.description: The ID of the target to submit the job to, as returned by the azure-quantum-get-providers tool.

shots.description: The number of shots to use for the job.

languageModelTools.name: azure-quantum-get-active-workspace
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the id of the active workspace for this conversation. Call this when you need to know what workspace is the active workspace being used in the context of the current conversation, for example when a customer asks 'What is the workspace that's being used?'

languageModelTools.name: azure-quantum-set-active-workspace
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Set the active workspace for this conversation by id. Call this when you need to set what workspace is the active workspace being used in the context of the current conversation, for example when a customer says 'Please use this workspace for my requests.'

workspace_id.description: The id of the workspace to set as active.

languageModelTools.name: azure-quantum-get-providers
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get a list of hardware providers available to the customer, along with their provided targets. Call this when you need to know what providers or targets are available, for example when a customer asks 'What are the available providers?' or 'What targets do I have available?'

languageModelTools.name: azure-quantum-get-target
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the target information for a specified target given its id. Call this whenever you need to know information about a specific target, for example when a customer asks 'What is the status of this target?'

target_id.description: The ID of the target to get.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-run-program
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Executes a Q# or OpenQASM program. The path to a .qs or .qasm file must be specified in the `filePath` parameter. A quantum simulator will be run locally. Q# or OpenQASM do not provide any CLI tools, so call this tool whenever you need to execute code, instead of running any CLI commands. The `shots` parameter controls the number of times to repeat the simulation. If the number of shots is greater than 1, a histogram will be generated, and the results will be displayed in a dedicated panel.

filePath.description: The absolute path to the .qs or .qasm file. If this file is part of a project, the whole project will be compiled as the program. A .qs file belongs to a project if it resides anywhere under a src/ directory whose parent directory also contains a qsharp.json manifest.

shots.description: The number of times to run the program. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-generate-circuit
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Generates a visual circuit diagram for a Q# or OpenQASM program. The path to a .qs or .qasm file must be specified in the `filePath` parameter. This tool will compile the program and generate a quantum circuit diagram that visually represents the quantum operations in the code. If the program contains any dynamic behavior such as comparing measurement results, then the program will be executed using a quantum simulator, and the resulting circuit diagram will simply be a trace of the operations that were actually executed during simulation. The diagram will be displayed in a dedicated circuit panel.

filePath.description: The absolute path to the .qs or .qasm file. If this file is part of a project, the whole project will be compiled as the program. A .qs file belongs to a project if it resides anywhere under a src/ directory whose parent directory also contains a qsharp.json manifest.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-run-resource-estimator
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs the quantum resource estimator on a Q# or OpenQASM program to calculate the required physical resources. The path to a .qs or .qasm file must be specified in the `filePath` parameter. This tool will analyze the Q# program and generate estimates of the quantum resources required to run the algorithm, such as the number of qubits, T gates, and other quantum operations. Results will be displayed in a dedicated resource estimator panel.

filePath.description: The absolute path to the .qs or .qasm file. If this file is part of a project, the whole project will be compiled as the program. A .qs file belongs to a project if it resides anywhere under a src/ directory whose parent directory also contains a qsharp.json manifest.

qubitTypes.description:
Array of qubit type labels to use for resource estimation. Allowed values:
- 'qubit_gate_ns_e3': Superconducting/spin qubit with 1e-3 error rate
- 'qubit_gate_ns_e4': Superconducting/spin qubit with 1e-4 error rate
- 'qubit_gate_us_e3': Trapped ion qubit with 1e-3 error rate
- 'qubit_gate_us_e4': Trapped ion qubit with 1e-4 error rate
- 'qubit_maj_ns_e4 + surface_code': Majorana qubit with 1e-4 error rate (surface code QEC)
- 'qubit_maj_ns_e6 + surface_code': Majorana qubit with 1e-6 error rate (surface code QEC)
- 'qubit_maj_ns_e4 + floquet_code': Majorana qubit with 1e-4 error rate (floquet code QEC)
- 'qubit_maj_ns_e6 + floquet_code': Majorana qubit with 1e-6 error rate (floquet code QEC)

errorBudget.description: Error budget for the resource estimation. Must be a number between 0 and 1. Default is 0.001.

languageModelTools.name: qsharp-get-library-descriptions
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns a Markdown string summarizing all Q# standard library items, grouped by namespace. Each entry includes its signature and a short description extracted from doc comments when available. Use this tool to answer any questions about Q# library functions or to ensure generated Q# code uses library functions correctly.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-learning-show
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Show the current learning activity. Opens the interactive Katas panel for lessons and exercises, or the source file for examples. Use to start or resume a learning session.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-learning-get-state
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read the current learning position and progress without opening the panel. Use to sync with the user's current position.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-learning-get-progress
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Return the full per-unit progress breakdown with activity-level completion status.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-learning-list-units
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List all available units with completion status and a recommended flag for the next suggested unit. Optionally filter by courseId.

courseId.description: Optional course ID to filter units. Omit to list units across all courses.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-learning-next
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Move to the next part in the learning sequence. The panel updates automatically.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-learning-previous
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Move to the previous part in the learning sequence. The panel updates automatically.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-learning-goto
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Jump to a specific course, unit, and optionally an activity. Use courseId + unitId from list-units or get-state. courseId defaults to 'katas' (Quantum Katas).

courseId.description: ID of the course. Defaults to 'katas' (built-in Quantum Katas). Use a folder name from qdk-learning-content for filesystem courses.

activityId.description: Activity ID within the unit. Omit to jump to the first activity.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-learning-run
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run the Q# code at the current position (exercise or example). Returns execution events and result.

shots.description: Number of times to run the program. Defaults to 1.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-learning-read-code
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read the user's current Q# code for the active exercise or example. Returns code text and file path without opening the panel.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-learning-check
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Check the student's solution against the test harness. Marks the exercise complete on pass. Only valid on exercises.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-learning-hint
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Return the built-in hints and solution explanation for the current exercise. Only valid on exercises.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-learning-solution
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Return the full reference solution code for the current exercise. Only valid on exercises.

languageModelTools.name: qdk-learning-reset
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reset the current exercise to its placeholder code and clear its completion. Destructive — requires confirmation. Only valid on exercises.

scripts.pretest: npm exec --no playwright install chromium && node ./test/buildTests.mjs
scripts.tsc:check: npm run tsc:check:main && npm run tsc:check:view && npm run tsc:check:learning
